title: Descriptor-stamp replay without forced 64-bit DMA mask

kernel.head: 7adf8f1abf95c0b7834e717bef1558aae4477e3e
kernel.subject: mmc: replay A733 descriptor stamps without DMA mask

observations.card_progress: SDXC detected and mmcblk0 created before the forced CMD18 IDMA read.
observations.descriptor_allocation: Removing the A733 64-bit dma_set_mask_and_coherent diagnostic path did not relocate the coherent descriptor ring: sg_dma remained 0x00000000fe100000 and DLBA remained 0x3f840000.
observations.data_buffer_allocation: The read data buffer moved below 4 GiB: sg0 dma=0x00000000fb800000 and descriptor buffer word became 0x3ee00000.
observations.after_cpu_sync: csum=0x22ebb674 d0 and d1 unchanged; OWN remained set; IDST=0x4000; CHDA=DLBA=0x3f840000; CBDA=0; CBCR=0x400; BBCR=0.

interpretation: The forced 64-bit DMA-mask diagnostic is not required to reproduce the descriptor-fetch stall.
A data buffer above 4 GiB is not required to reproduce the stall, because H018 moved the data buffer below 4 GiB and still failed before CBDA became nonzero.
H018 did not actually test a different descriptor-ring physical window because the coherent descriptor allocation stayed at 0xfe100000.
The next proof must directly change the descriptor allocation/access class, not another visible SDMMC register or DMA mask guess.

next_hypothesis.title: Streaming-mapped descriptor ring outside coherent CMA
next_hypothesis.summary: Allocate the descriptor ring from normal memory and map it with dma_map_single plus explicit syncs. If IDMAC consumes that ring, coherent/CMA descriptor visibility is the suspect. If it still stalls unchanged, move below descriptor memory class toward SDMMC0 master/fabric reachability.
